question.text: In the context of commodity futures, 'going short' without any existing long position means:

answer.text: Selling a futures contract in anticipation of price decrease before expiry
Buying a futures contract expecting prices to rise
Taking physical delivery of the commodity

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Going short means selling a futures contract without holding the underlying or an offsetting long position, in anticipation of price decrease. If prices fall, the short seller profits. **The Trap:** Buying futures is going long. Closing a long is squaring off. Taking delivery is settlement. Shorting is establishing a new sell position expecting price decline. **Cross-Exam Context:** Short selling in futures is covered across Series VIII and I. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Short sellers must be aware of delivery obligations — if they hold the short position through expiry, they must deliver the physical commodity or face default penalties.
